attack_surface.data_description: Transactions (Asset 19) signed with the User's private key (Asset 18) after the key has been compromised.

security_gap.gap_description: If a User's private keys are compromised, they may attempt to repudiate transactions made by an attacker using those keys, claiming they did not authorize them.

actor_risk.description: The security of the User's private keys is paramount for non-repudiation of their actions on the blockchain.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Key compromise undermines the cryptographic proof of transaction origin, leading to disputes.

assumptions: Private keys can be stolen or unwillingly disclosed.
The blockchain itself correctly attributes signed transactions to the key that signed them.
The legitimate user will want to deny actions taken by an attacker with their keys.

mitigation.strategy: Strong user education on private key security. Use of hardware wallets to make key exfiltration harder. While true cryptographic repudiation is not possible if the signature is valid, mitigating key compromise reduces the likelihood of such disputes.

description: If a User's private keys (Asset 18) are compromised (related to Behavior 104), the User might later attempt to repudiate malicious transactions (Asset 19) made by an attacker, even though the transactions are cryptographically signed by their key.
